title: Pawn Structure
text: Home * Evaluation * Pawn Structure Elke Rehder , Chain of Pawns [1] Pawn structure is a term used to describe the positions of all the pawns on the board, ignoring all other pieces. Pawn structure encompasses a broad range of ideas, from the general shape of the pawns (such as closed or open) to specific characteristics of individual pawns. Contents 1 Hashing Pawns 2 Standard Terms 2.1 Single Pawn 2.2 Groups of Pawns 2.3 General 3 Dependent on Pawn Structure 4 See also 5 Publications 6 Forum Posts 6.1 2000 ... 6.2 2010 ... 6.3 2015 ... 6.4 2020 ... 7 External Links 8 References Hashing Pawns [ edit ] see main article Pawn Hash Table Full evaluation of pawn structure might be quite expensive. To speed up the evaluation, many programs use a separate pawn hash table , addressed by a special key derived from the pawn's (and possibly also king's) position. Since pawn structure changes rather slowly, hit rate for such table is typically above 95%. Anything strictly pawn related can be stored in this hash table, including pawn shield terms to be used dynamically for king safety .
